goal: Continuously improve automated trading strategy performance on BTCUSDT via MCP dev_space1. Maximize net PnL and trade count while minimizing max drawdown. Run research cycles indefinitely, exploring new features, models, and strategies, validating them against the active-signal-v1 baseline (Sharpe 1.06, +13% return, 498 trades, max DD 2.4%).
principles: Only genuinely new information dimensions can add edge — vol_term_spread succeeded because vol term structure was new
Transformations of already-captured signals (price level, vol level, momentum level, time) produce redundancy not alpha
Volume-based features are the largest untapped information source — test them first
Momentum acceleration (2nd derivative) is fundamentally different from momentum level (RSI, 1st derivative)
Standalone test before integration — never skip standalone validation of a new feature
Features must show ≥5% improvement in ≥2 of {PnL, trades, DD} vs baseline to promote
Integration test is mandatory — standalone edge does NOT guarantee integration improvement
ATR-based regime features are dead — correlated with existing IV and vol features
Session/time-weighted returns are dead — insufficient edge for systematic trading
Higher-order return statistics (skewness, kurtosis) are dead — noise generators at 5m
cumulative_summary: After v2: 1 feature PROMOTED (cf_vol_term_spread: $1496 PnL, Sharpe 1.456, 330 trades, DD 1.68%). 5 features REJECTED across v1-v2: cf_choppiness_index (ATR regime = correlated with vol features), cf_session_momentum (no edge, -$43), cf_return_skew (noise), cf_funding_extreme (no 5m edge). cf_cumulative_delta pending backtest (MCP failure in v2). KEY INSIGHT: only genuinely new information dimensions add edge; transformations of already-captured signals fail. Volume is the largest completely untapped information source. Momentum 2nd derivative is also unexplored (RSI is 1st derivative only). v3 tests these 3 dimensions.

tasks.theory: Confirm system health, verify cf_cumulative_delta is still published from v2, establish gold collection state

steps.instruction: Load mandatory MCP guidance and bootstrap context. Read all 10 mandatory rules and research rules.
Validate all 46 tools are healthy before starting research cycle. Must show healthy_domains=10.
List available features — verify cf_cumulative_delta is published from v2 attempt. Count total features and note all cf_* columns.
Check current gold collection state to confirm cf_vol_term_spread entry exists.
Open or create research project for plan_v3 cycle.
Record v3 plan hypothesis: testing 3 new orthogonal dimensions — cumulative_delta (order flow), trend_acceleration (momentum 2nd derivative), relative_volume (volume anomaly).

args.title: Plan v3 hypothesis
args.body: Three untouched dimensions: (1) cf_cumulative_delta — order flow pressure, retry from v2 MCP failure, (2) cf_trend_acceleration — ROC(12)-ROC(48) momentum acceleration, genuinely different from RSI, (3) cf_relative_volume — volume/SMA(volume) anomaly detection, no volume signal exists. All three are orthogonal to baseline features and to each other.

tasks.theory: Volume × directional body proxy measures buying/selling pressure. Published in v2 but never backtested due to MCP corruption. If order flow direction confirms baseline signals, it should improve entry quality (higher PF, better WR) potentially at cost of fewer but better trades.

steps.instruction: Build feature dataset to ensure cf_cumulative_delta column is available. Use action='build' (not 'refresh') to guarantee new columns are computed.
Clone baseline snapshot for cf_cumulative_delta standalone test. Name the new snapshot 'test_cumdelta_v3'.

Validate signal logic with cf_cumulative_delta gate added to baseline. signal_long: dow in [0,1,2,3,6] and sum([iv_est in [3,4], rsi in [1], hour in [6], cl_1h in [2]])>=3 and cf_cumulative_delta > 0. signal_short: dow in [3] and sum([cl_1d in [2], cl_15m in [3], cl_1h in [1], hour in [15]])>=2 and cf_cumulative_delta < 0.

args.signal_long: dow in [0,1,2,3,6] and sum([iv_est in [3,4], rsi in [1], hour in [6], cl_1h in [2]])>=3 and cf_cumulative_delta > 0
args.signal_short: dow in [3] and sum([cl_1d in [2], cl_15m in [3], cl_1h in [1], hour in [15]])>=2 and cf_cumulative_delta < 0

steps.instruction: Save snapshot version 2 with cf_cumulative_delta filter. Use the snapshot_id returned from s1_clone. Set signal_long and signal_short as validated above.

args.signal_long: dow in [0,1,2,3,6] and sum([iv_est in [3,4], rsi in [1], hour in [6], cl_1h in [2]])>=3 and cf_cumulative_delta > 0
args.signal_short: dow in [3] and sum([cl_1d in [2], cl_15m in [3], cl_1h in [1], hour in [15]])>=2 and cf_cumulative_delta < 0

steps.instruction: Preflight readiness check for cf_cumulative_delta standalone backtest.
Run full-history backtest for cf_cumulative_delta standalone test. Full 2023-2024 period.
Inspect backtest results. Extract: net_pnl, trade_count, win_rate, profit_factor, max_drawdown_pct, sharpe_ratio. Compare vs baseline ($1308.65, 498 trades, DD 2.4%, Sharpe 1.06).
Record cf_cumulative_delta standalone results to research memory. Include all metrics. Verdict: PROMOTE if ≥5% better in ≥2 metrics vs baseline; WATCHLIST if ≥3% in 1 metric; REJECT otherwise.

tasks.theory: Momentum 2nd derivative (ROC_short - ROC_long smoothed) captures acceleration/deceleration of momentum. RSI measures momentum level (1st derivative). Trend direction captures sign. Acceleration captures whether momentum is building or fading — a bearish divergence signal when price rising but acceleration falling. Genuinely new information dimension.

steps.instruction: Validate cf_trend_acceleration feature. This computes the difference between short-term (12-bar) and long-term (48-bar) rate of change, smoothed over 24 bars. Use ctx.series() API only.

args.name: cf_trend_acceleration
args.description: Momentum acceleration: difference between 12-bar and 48-bar rate of change, smoothed over 24 bars. Positive = momentum accelerating upward, negative = decelerating or accelerating downward.

steps.instruction: Publish cf_trend_acceleration to the feature registry.
Build feature dataset to compute cf_trend_acceleration column.
Clone baseline snapshot for cf_trend_acceleration standalone test. Name the new snapshot 'test_trend_accel_v3'.

Validate signal logic with cf_trend_acceleration gate. signal_long: dow in [0,1,2,3,6] and sum([iv_est in [3,4], rsi in [1], hour in [6], cl_1h in [2]])>=3 and cf_trend_acceleration > 0. signal_short: dow in [3] and sum([cl_1d in [2], cl_15m in [3], cl_1h in [1], hour in [15]])>=2 and cf_trend_acceleration < 0.

args.signal_long: dow in [0,1,2,3,6] and sum([iv_est in [3,4], rsi in [1], hour in [6], cl_1h in [2]])>=3 and cf_trend_acceleration > 0
args.signal_short: dow in [3] and sum([cl_1d in [2], cl_15m in [3], cl_1h in [1], hour in [15]])>=2 and cf_trend_acceleration < 0

steps.instruction: Save snapshot version 2 with cf_trend_acceleration filter.

args.signal_long: dow in [0,1,2,3,6] and sum([iv_est in [3,4], rsi in [1], hour in [6], cl_1h in [2]])>=3 and cf_trend_acceleration > 0
args.signal_short: dow in [3] and sum([cl_1d in [2], cl_15m in [3], cl_1h in [1], hour in [15]])>=2 and cf_trend_acceleration < 0

Run full-history backtest for cf_trend_acceleration standalone.
Inspect backtest results. Compare vs baseline ($1308.65, 498 trades, DD 2.4%, Sharpe 1.06).

results_table_rows.run_id: N/A - MCP session lost before backtest

tasks.theory: Volume intensity anomaly (current volume / 96-bar SMA) detects unusual market participation. High relative volume often coincides with meaningful moves and institutional activity. The entire volume dimension is completely untapped — no feature in the system uses volume at all. This adds genuinely new information that price-based features cannot capture.

steps.instruction: Validate cf_relative_volume feature. Computes ratio of current volume to its 96-bar moving average. Use ctx.series() API only.

args.name: cf_relative_volume
args.code:
def compute(ctx):
    volume = ctx.series('volume')
    vol_ma = volume.rolling(96).mean()
    return volume / vol_ma
args.description: Volume intensity anomaly: current volume divided by 96-bar SMA of volume. Values > 1.0 = above-average participation. Detects volume spikes that often precede or confirm significant price moves.

steps.instruction: Publish cf_relative_volume to the feature registry.
Build feature dataset to compute cf_relative_volume column.
Clone baseline snapshot for cf_relative_volume standalone test. Name the new snapshot 'test_rel_volume_v3'.

Validate signal logic with cf_relative_volume gate. For BOTH long and short, require cf_relative_volume > 1.0 (above-average volume confirms the signal). signal_long: dow in [0,1,2,3,6] and sum([iv_est in [3,4], rsi in [1], hour in [6], cl_1h in [2]])>=3 and cf_relative_volume > 1.0. signal_short: dow in [3] and sum([cl_1d in [2], cl_15m in [3], cl_1h in [1], hour in [15]])>=2 and cf_relative_volume > 1.0.

args.signal_long: dow in [0,1,2,3,6] and sum([iv_est in [3,4], rsi in [1], hour in [6], cl_1h in [2]])>=3 and cf_relative_volume > 1.0
args.signal_short: dow in [3] and sum([cl_1d in [2], cl_15m in [3], cl_1h in [1], hour in [15]])>=2 and cf_relative_volume > 1.0

args.signal_short: dow in [3] and sum([cl_1d in [2], cl_15m in [3], cl_1h in [1], hour in [15]])>=2 and cf_relative_volume > 1.0

steps.instruction: Preflight readiness check.
Run full-history backtest for cf_relative_volume standalone.
Inspect backtest results. Compare vs baseline ($1308.65, 498 trades, DD 2.4%, Sharpe 1.06).
Record cf_relative_volume results. PROMOTE if ≥5% better in ≥2 metrics; WATCHLIST if ≥3% in 1; REJECT otherwise.

tasks.theory: Combine all PROMOTED/WATCHLIST features from stages 1-3 with the already-promoted cf_vol_term_spread. Test integrated strategy vs baseline to verify no cannibalization. Walk-forward and ownership analysis to confirm robustness.

steps.instruction: Clone baseline snapshot for integration test. Name it 'integrated_v3'. This snapshot will combine cf_vol_term_spread + all promoted features from stages 1-3.

Compose integration signal logic. Start with baseline and add ALL promoted features (from stage 1-3 results) as AND gates. MUST also include cf_vol_term_spread > 0.05 / < -0.05 from v1. If a feature was REJECTED, do NOT include it. Use the actual verdicts reported by workers in stages 1-3 to decide which features to include. Build signal_long and signal_short strings accordingly. Validate with backtests_strategy_validate.
Save integration snapshot version 2 with composed signal logic from s4_compose. Include all promoted feature gates.
Run full-history backtest for integrated strategy.

Inspect integration backtest results. Must show simultaneous improvement in PnL, trades, AND drawdown vs baseline ($1308.65, 498 trades, DD 2.4%) AND vs cf_vol_term_spread standalone ($1496.34, 330 trades, DD 1.68%).
Run walk-forward analysis on integrated strategy to verify out-of-sample stability.
Run signal ownership analysis to verify which features carry profit and whether any promoted feature cannibalizes others.

Record integration results. If integrated strategy beats baseline on all 3 metrics (PnL, trades, DD): add to gold collection with gold_collection(action='add'). Record full metrics and feature breakdown. If not improved: record as negative result explaining which feature caused degradation.
